sourceType: Web scan (protocol docs, analytics, technical articles)
scanType: Focused scan: Perps DEX architecture (closest technical analog to LEVER)
summary: Hyperliquid: custom L1, $200B+ monthly volume, HLP vault at $380M TVL (6.93% APR). Active market-making vault (not passive). HLP generated 42% lifetime CAGR but dropped to 22% trailing 12-month. Sharpe ratio 5.2. Key risk: $4M HLP loss from trader exploiting liquidation mechanics.
GMX: Oracle-based pricing (no orderbook). GLP holders are direct counterparty to traders. No active hedging. More transparent and stable but less responsive. Original 'real yield' DeFi model.

Architecture is the moat, not features or tokenomics. Key lesson: execution speed, UI, and incentives only amplify what the core architecture allows. If the model mismanages liquidity, concentrates risk, or depends on perfect market conditions, no optimization fixes it.
On-chain perpetuals hit $1.5T+ monthly volume (Oct 2025), 18% of global derivatives market, up 52% YoY. Three architectural camps: orderbook (Hyperliquid, Lighter), AMM (GMX), and hybrid.

LEVER's LeverVault is closer to GMX's GLP than Hyperliquid's HLP: LP as counterparty, oracle-based pricing (PI), no active hedging by vault. But LEVER adds time-based risk compression and borrow fees that GMX lacks. LEVER's design is a hybrid informed by both.

entities.name: Hyperliquid
entities.role: Custom L1 perps DEX, $200B+ monthly volume, HLP vault $380M TVL

entities.name: HLP (Hyperliquidity Provider)
entities.role: Active market-making vault, 42% lifetime CAGR, $4M exploit loss incident

entities.name: GMX
entities.role: AMM-based perps DEX, GLP counterparty model, oracle pricing

entities.name: GLP
entities.role: Passive LP-as-counterparty vault, direct PnL transfer between traders and LPs

relationships.relationship: similar model: LP as counterparty, oracle pricing. LEVER adds time-based compression + borrow fees.
different model: HLP is active market-making, LEVER is passive LP counterparty.
lesson: liquidation mechanics can be gamed. LEVER's partial liquidation + insurance fund are defenses.

fromHyperliquid: Active vault strategies generate higher returns (42% CAGR) but introduce opacity and exploit risk ($4M loss).
LEVER's passive LP model is simpler and more transparent. This is a feature, not a limitation.
WebSocket-first data architecture enables sub-second execution. LEVER's oracle should use WebSocket.
Purpose-built execution environment matters. Base L2 gives LEVER low fees + Coinbase ecosystem.
fromGMX: Oracle-based pricing (like LEVER's PI) avoids orderbook manipulation but creates oracle dependency risk.
LP-as-counterparty means LPs profit when traders lose and vice versa. LEVER's borrow fee 'ticking clock' tilts this in LP favor over time.
GLP's simplicity (deposit, earn real yield) drove massive adoption. LeverVault should be equally simple.
GMX proved 'real yield' narrative works for attracting LP capital. LEVER's 175-400% projected APY is the same narrative on steroids.
universalLessons: Architecture > features > tokenomics. Get the core model right first.
Liquidation mechanics are the #1 exploit surface. LEVER's partial liquidation + insurance fund are correct.
LP returns must be real (from fees), not subsidized (from emissions). LEVER's deterministic fee split ensures this.
Risk transparency builds trust and retention. LEVER should publish real-time vault PnL, utilization, and risk metrics.

workstreamTags.BUILD: LEVER's LeverVault is closest to GMX's GLP model (passive LP counterparty, oracle pricing) but with LEVER-specific innovations (time-based compression, borrow fees, ERC-4626). This is the RIGHT architecture. Key lessons: (1) Liquidation mechanics are the #1 exploit surface, verify thoroughly. (2) Real-time vault metrics (PnL, utilization) should be exposed from day one. (3) WebSocket-first for oracle data.
workstreamTags.CEO: For investors familiar with DeFi: 'LEVER is the GMX for prediction markets.' GMX proved LP-as-counterparty with oracle pricing at scale ($200B+ perps market). LEVER applies the same proven model to the $21B prediction market sector, adding time-based risk compression and borrow fees that make LP returns more predictable.
workstreamTags.ADVISOR: On-chain perps hit $1.5T+ monthly, 18% of global derivatives. The model is proven. LEVER's innovation is applying it to prediction market outcomes instead of crypto spot prices. The architecture translates directly.
workstreamTags.IMPROVE: Hyperliquid's UI is the gold standard for perps. Study their position management, PnL display, and vault deposit UX for LeverVault design.
